task_desc: Fix frame-rate-dependent animation interpolations and groom-state location amnesia -- (1) four animation variables in updateAnimForBehavior() and drawFlyBody() increment per-frame without dt scaling, causing animations to run 2x faster on 120Hz displays vs 60Hz: anim.wingSpread interpolation at main.js:600 (uses += (target - current) * 0.15), anim.proboscisExtend at main.js:607 (uses += (target - current) * 0.1), anim.groomPhase at main.js:611 (uses += 0.12), and anim.walkPhase at main.js:829 (uses += spd * 0.5); fix by passing dtScale to updateAnimForBehavior() and using exponential interpolation for lerps (e.g. Math.pow(0.85, dtScale) for wingSpread) and linear dt scaling for phase accumulators (groomPhase += 0.12 * dtScale, walkPhase += spd * 0.5 * dtScale), (2) groom-state location amnesia: the groom animation in drawLegs() at main.js:1156 reads BRAIN.stimulate.touchLocation live each frame to decide which legs animate, but touchResetFrame clears touchLocation to null after ~2 seconds (main.js:1372) while the groom behavior state persists for its minimum duration of 2000ms and often longer; once cleared, groomLoc falls back to 'thorax' (line 1156) causing a jarring mid-groom animation switch; similarly drawAbdomen() at main.js:933 checks touchLocation for the abdomen curl which stops abruptly when cleared; fix by storing the trigger touchLocation in the behavior state object when entering groom state and reading from there instead of the live stimulus. Files: js/main.js.
